name: Christopher McCandless
title: Last known photo of Christopher McCandless, a self-portrait taken in August 1992 outside the abandoned bus where he died in the Alaskan wilderness.
description: Christopher McCandless took this self-portrait outside an abandoned Fairbanks city bus on the Stampede Trail in the Alaska wilderness in August 1992. McCandless had given away his savings, abandoned his car, and hitchhiked to Alaska to live off the land — a journey later chronicled in Jon Krakauer's book "Into the Wild". After surviving for over 100 days, he became too weak to travel, likely due to starvation after accidentally consuming toxic seeds that prevented his body from absorbing nutrients. His body was found on September 6, 1992, by moose hunters. He was 24 years old. A note on the bus door read: "S.O.S. I need your help. I am injured, near death, and too weak to hike out."
bio: Christopher McCandless was an American adventurer who gave away his savings and hitchhiked to Alaska to live in the wilderness. His story was immortalized in Jon Krakauer's book and Sean Penn's film "Into the Wild".
